name: Community Action Partnership Of Hennepin County
description_short: Non-profit anti-poverty organization in Hennepin County offering free financial counseling, employment readiness, and emergency assistance programs to low-income residents.
description_long:
Community Action Partnership of Hennepin County (CAP-HC) is a non-profit organization dedicated to disrupting poverty cycles in Hennepin County, Minnesota. The organization serves residents living at or below 200% of the Federal Poverty Level, with a mission to help people move from survival to prosperity through comprehensive wraparound services. With approximately 1 in 10 Hennepin County residents living below the Federal Poverty Line, CAP-HC addresses both immediate financial crises and long-term economic mobility.

CAP-HC provides a diverse portfolio of free and low-cost services spanning emergency financial assistance, employment support, financial education, and housing services. Their offerings include energy and water bill assistance, rental assistance, vehicle repair support, tax filing resources, MNsure application help, employment readiness programs, financial wellness counseling and workshops, homebuyer education and counseling, and job search assistance. The organization served over 20,000 households in 2025 across these programs, demonstrating significant community reach and impact.

CAP-HC distinguishes itself through its holistic, poverty-focused approach rather than credit-based services. The organization combines immediate crisis intervention (utility bills, rent, vehicle repairs) with skill-building and long-term financial independence programs (employment training, financial literacy, homebuyer counseling). They also provide culturally responsive services with materials and support available in 10+ languages including Arabic, Hmong, Somali, Spanish, Vietnamese, and Ukrainian, reflecting Hennepin County's diversity.

CAP-HC is best suited for low-income Hennepin County residents facing immediate financial hardship combined with a desire for long-term economic stability. The eligibility requirements (below 200% Federal Poverty Level, 18+, able to work full-time for employment programs) are transparent and accessible. The main caveat is that services are geographically limited to Hennepin County residents only, and employment readiness programs require full-time work availability, excluding some populations.
pros: Completely free services—no fees for counseling, workshops, or financial assistance programs
Multi-language support in 10+ languages including Somali, Hmong, Arabic, Vietnamese, and Ukrainian
Comprehensive wraparound approach addressing immediate needs (utilities, rent, repairs) plus long-term wealth building (employment, homeownership, financial literacy)
Transparent income eligibility guidelines published on website with clear maximum income thresholds by household size
High service volume in 2025 (19,616 energy/water services, 466 housing services, 304 financial wellness services) demonstrates proven operational capacity
Free homebuyer counseling and workshops for path to homeownership, not just credit repair
Employment readiness program explicitly focuses on 'living-wage jobs' not just any employment
cons: Services limited to Hennepin County residents only—no help available for consumers in other Minnesota counties or states
Employment readiness program requires ability and willingness to work full-time, excluding disabled individuals, caregivers, or those seeking part-time work
No credit repair or credit-building services offered—cannot help consumers dispute credit report errors or rebuild damaged credit
Limited information on program wait times, service appointment availability, or how quickly assistance can be accessed during emergencies
Website does not specify maximum assistance amounts for rental, energy, or water bill programs, creating uncertainty about aid sufficiency
best_for: Low-income Hennepin County residents facing immediate utility bill, rent, or vehicle repair crises who also want financial counseling and budget training
Job seekers earning below 200% of federal poverty level who need resume help, interview coaching, and employment readiness support
First-time homebuyers with limited income seeking free homebuyer education and counseling to build homeownership readiness
Non-English speakers from refugee or immigrant communities who need services in their native language with culturally responsive support
diagnosis: CAP-HC is best for low-income Hennepin County residents facing immediate financial crises (utilities, rent, repairs) combined with a desire to build long-term economic independence through employment, financial literacy, or homeownership. The main caveat is strict geographic limitation to Hennepin County and income/work capacity requirements that exclude some vulnerable populations; additionally, this organization provides no credit repair, credit-building, or credit counseling services for consumers with credit report problems.
services: Energy Assistance Program—help paying gas and electric bills for households spending 25%+ of income on utilities
Vehicle Repair Assistance—financial help for vehicle repairs to ensure reliable transportation to employment
Rental Assistance—one-time payment for eligible households to catch up on rent or pay security deposits
Water Assistance—help paying down water bill balances for households behind on payments
Free Tax Filing Resources—no-cost and low-cost tax preparation assistance
MNsure Application Assistance—help completing health insurance applications to access low or no-cost coverage
Employment Readiness Program—resume support, interview training, job search assistance, and living-wage job placement
Financial Wellness Workshops and Counseling—budgeting, money management, and financial goal-setting education
Homebuyer Workshops—comprehensive homeownership education and planning
Homebuyer Counseling—free one-on-one counseling for prospective homebuyers
Multilingual support in 10+ languages including Spanish, Somali, Hmong, Arabic, Vietnamese, Ukrainian, Chinese, French, Portuguese, Korean, and English
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
address: 7101 Northland Cir N, Suite 123, Brooklyn Park, MN 55428
